description:
GOAL: Tree-walking interpreter for fz-IR. Critical correctness oracle: lets us validate .11.4 (AST->IR) end-to-end without dragging Cranelift in.

## Decisions captured

- Walks fz-IR directly; mirrors existing interp semantics on Value.
- Bridges with the existing Rc<Value>-based interp at boundaries: IR runs a fn, returns a Value.
- Uses the same builtin set as the interp.
- Without this we'd be debugging fz-IR + Cranelift simultaneously, which is hell. Critical infra.

## Deliverables (new src/ir_interp.rs)

- pub fn run_ir_fn(module: &ir::Module, fn_id: FnId, args: Vec<Value>) -> Result<Value, String>
- Implements Stmt + Term semantics:
  - Let evaluates Prim, binds Var.
  - Goto jumps to block, threads block params.
  - If branches.
  - Call invokes another fn, captures continuation.
  - Return calls current frame's continuation.
  - Halt returns the result to caller.
- Trampoline-style: top-level driver loops until Halt.

## Tests

- Run each fixture in fixtures/ through both Rc<Value> interp and IR interp; assert results identical.
- Multi-clause dispatch (classify, fact, fib).
- Recursion (count, sum).
- Higher-order (apply2, compose, vec_map).
- Pattern matching across all Pattern variants.
- Macros + modules (post-expand + post-resolve correctness).

## Out of scope

- Tier-up integration (IR interp purely for IR validation; runtime fz programs go through Cranelift codegen via .11.7+).
- Performance — this is for correctness, not speed.
title: fz-IR interpreter (debug oracle)
